revision.text:
{{used in system}} {{cascade-protected template|page=module}} {{Module rating |release}} This module directly imports all functions from the {{luaref|Ustring library|mw.ustring}} library. Documentation for each function can be found there. The module takes an indefinite number of arguments. Arguments given as {{para|s1}}, {{para|s2}}, etc, are read first, and are used as strings. All remaining numerical arguments are coerced to number type if possible, and remain strings if not. If you wish for a numerical (i.e. unnamed or "{{para|1}}", "{{para|2}}") to remain a string, you can simply escape it by inserting \ at the beginning of the string. Note that MediaWiki will always trim whitespace from named arguments; to give arguments with surrounding whitespace you must use unnamed parameters starting with \. {| class="wikitable" |+ How to pass inconvenient strings |- ! To pass this... !! Write this !! Explanation |- | " 0123 " || {{para||\ 123 }} || To get surrounding whitespace must use unnamed, but must precede with \ to indicate that it isn't a number. |- | "0123" || {{para|s1|0123}} || If you don't need to preserve whitespace use {{para|s1}} etc. |- | "0123" || {{para|1|\0123}} || If you don't need to preserve whitespace explicitly use {{para|1}} etc. |- | In another template, to pass its parameter {{{1}}}, preserving whitespace || {{para||\{{{1}}}}} || Must provide the \ with unknown string input. |- | In another template, to pass its parameter {{{1}}}, stripping whitespace || {{para|s1|{{{1}}}}} || |- | In another template, to pass its parameter {{{1}}}, stripping whitespace || {{para|1|\{{#if:1|{{{1}}}}}}} || |} You can also wrap results in tags. All other unused arguments will be passed to {{luaref|frame:extensionTag}} == Usage == {{#invoke:{{BASEPAGENAME}}|''function_name''|arg1|arg2|...}} is equivalent to {{luaref|Ustring library|mw.ustring.''function_name''|args=arg1, arg2, ...}} ===Example using mw.ustring.sub=== {{#invoke:Ustring|sub|s1=abcde|2|4}} produces: {{#invoke:Ustring|sub|s1=abcde|2|4}} ===Example using mw.ustring.gsub=== {{#invoke:Ustring|gsub|s1=1234|23|}} produces: {{#invoke:Ustring|gsub|s1=1234|23|}} ===Example using mw.ustring.char=== &#{{#invoke:ustring|char|49|48|59}} produces: &#{{#invoke:ustring|char|49|48|59}} ===Example using mw.ustring.match=== {{#invoke:Ustring|match|s1=abcde|s2=(c%w)}} produces: {{#invoke:Ustring|match|s1=abcde|s2=(c%w)}} Note: Only the first match is returned. Additional returns are omitted because mw.ustring.gsub's second return value is generally undesirable. ===Example using tag arguments=== 
{{#invoke:Ustring|match
|s1={{Module:Ustring}}|%s%s%sif%snot%s[^%s]+%sthen.+%
%s%s%send
|tag=syntaxhighlight|lang=lua}}
produces: {{#invoke:Ustring|match |s1={{Module:Ustring}}|%s%s%sif%snot%s[^%s]+%sthen.+% %s%s%send |tag=syntaxhighlight|lang=lua}} Note that: {{#tag:pre|{{#invoke:Ustring|match |s1={{Module:Ustring}}|%s%s%sif%snot%s[^%s]+%sthen.+% %s%s%send}}}} produces: {{#invoke:Ustring|match |s1={{Module:Ustring}}|%s%s%sif%snot%s[^%s]+%sthen.+% %s%s%send}} == Errors == Errors from accessing {{luaref|Ustring library|mw.ustring}} should be maintained, e.g.: {{#invoke:Ustring|xyzzy}} should produce: {{script error|Script error: The function "xyzzy" does not exist.}} and {{#invoke:Ustring|maxPatternLength}} should produce: {{script error|Script error: "maxPatternLength" is not a function.}} == See also == {{String handling templates}} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox | | [[Category:Modules that manipulate strings|*]] }}

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was 1 revision imported: IMPORTING ALL WANTED MODULES
revision.text:
{{used in system}} {{cascade-protected template|page=module}} {{Module rating |release}} This module directly imports all functions from the {{luaref|Ustring library|mw.ustring}} library. Documentation for each function can be found there. The module takes an indefinite number of arguments. Arguments given as {{para|s1}}, {{para|s2}}, etc, are read first, and are used as strings. All remaining numerical arguments are coerced to number type if possible, and remain strings if not. If you wish for a numerical (i.e. unnamed or "{{para|1}}", "{{para|2}}") to remain a string, you can simply escape it by inserting \ at the beginning of the string. Note that MediaWiki will always trim whitespace from named arguments; to give arguments with surrounding whitespace you must use unnamed parameters starting with \. {| class="wikitable" |+ How to pass inconvenient strings |- ! To pass this... !! Write this !! Explanation |- | " 0123 " || {{para||\ 123 }} || To get surrounding whitespace must use unnamed, but must precede with \ to indicate that it isn't a number. |- | "0123" || {{para|s1|0123}} || If you don't need to preserve whitespace use {{para|s1}} etc. |- | "0123" || {{para|1|\0123}} || If you don't need to preserve whitespace explicitly use {{para|1}} etc. |- | In another template, to pass its parameter {{{1}}}, preserving whitespace || {{para||\{{{1}}}}} || Must provide the \ with unknown string input. |- | In another template, to pass its parameter {{{1}}}, stripping whitespace || {{para|s1|{{{1}}}}} || |- | In another template, to pass its parameter {{{1}}}, stripping whitespace || {{para|1|\{{#if:1|{{{1}}}}}}} || |} You can also wrap results in tags. All other unused arguments will be passed to {{luaref|frame:extensionTag}} == Usage == {{#invoke:{{BASEPAGENAME}}|''function_name''|arg1|arg2|...}} is equivalent to {{luaref|Ustring library|mw.ustring.''function_name''|args=arg1, arg2, ...}} ===Example using mw.ustring.sub=== {{#invoke:Ustring|sub|s1=abcde|2|4}} produces: {{#invoke:Ustring|sub|s1=abcde|2|4}} ===Example using mw.ustring.gsub=== {{#invoke:Ustring|gsub|s1=1234|23|}} produces: {{#invoke:Ustring|gsub|s1=1234|23|}} ===Example using mw.ustring.char=== &#{{#invoke:ustring|char|49|48|59}} produces: &#{{#invoke:ustring|char|49|48|59}} ===Example using mw.ustring.match=== {{#invoke:Ustring|match|s1=abcde|s2=(c%w)}} produces: {{#invoke:Ustring|match|s1=abcde|s2=(c%w)}} Note: Only the first match is returned. Additional returns are omitted because mw.ustring.gsub's second return value is generally undesirable. ===Example using tag arguments=== 
{{#invoke:Ustring|match
|s1={{Module:Ustring}}|%s%s%sif%snot%s[^%s]+%sthen.+%
%s%s%send
|tag=syntaxhighlight|lang=lua}}
produces: {{#invoke:Ustring|match |s1={{Module:Ustring}}|%s%s%sif%snot%s[^%s]+%sthen.+% %s%s%send |tag=syntaxhighlight|lang=lua}} Note that: {{#tag:pre|{{#invoke:Ustring|match |s1={{Module:Ustring}}|%s%s%sif%snot%s[^%s]+%sthen.+% %s%s%send}}}} produces: {{#invoke:Ustring|match |s1={{Module:Ustring}}|%s%s%sif%snot%s[^%s]+%sthen.+% %s%s%send}} == Errors == Errors from accessing {{luaref|Ustring library|mw.ustring}} should be maintained, e.g.: {{#invoke:Ustring|xyzzy}} should produce: {{script error|Script error: The function "xyzzy" does not exist.}} and {{#invoke:Ustring|maxPatternLength}} should produce: {{script error|Script error: "maxPatternLength" is not a function.}} == See also == {{String handling templates}} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox | | [[Category:Modules that manipulate strings|*]] }}
